data.body:
### [4/10] LEVER — narrow prev_snapshot to a KEY-SET + THE ONE OPEN GATE that blocks it

prev_snapshot is NOT redundant but is GROSSLY OVER-PROVISIONED. It stores the full ACLI field payload per issue (fetcher.py:413 + parent/comment/issuelinks enrichments, ~12 KB/issue, volatile fields updated/created/watches/worklog tick every pass) — but its live consumers need at MOST the SET of jira_keys seen last pass. Sizing trace (differ branches):
- Branch (1) inbound-create `_compute_mutations_emit_jira_only` reads prev ONLY via key-membership (`in_local = key in local_state`); zero field reads. THE sole non-deletable role.
- Branch (3) field-drift `_compute_mutations_emit_both`: reads full fields BUT its emitted (outbound,update) values are DROPPED at _mutation_to_batch_dict (batch_dispatch.py:107-110 looks for `changed_fields`; branch-3 writes a bare dict -> {} -> empty no-op update_issue). => prev does NOT need the 5 mirrored fields for drift.
- dual-identity invariant: inert (fetcher never writes local_id). rebar-id/phantom-guard: reads CURR labels not prev. seed_baselines: dead.
- So prev can shrink to a bare KEY-SET: 24 MB -> ~25 KB current, and version count 4450 -> ~membership-changes (volatile churn gone). Near-eliminates prev as a growth driver. NO delete, NO history rewrite, NO new ref.

SHAPE CONSTRAINT (dossier A): store the narrowed prev as a DICT `{jira_key: {}}`, NOT a literal `set` — check_dual_identity_complete and _emit_both's `local_state[key] or {}` guards assume dict entries.

*** THE ONE OPEN GATE — resolve BEFORE implementing (two subagents partially disagree): ***
Narrowing to a key-set is NOT equivalent to prev={}. With prev={} (no keys) every curr key routes to _emit_jira_only and _emit_both NEVER runs ("expensive but safe", reconcile.py:727). With a key-set {key:{}}, _emit_both RE-ACTIVATES with a NULL ancestor: for keys in BOTH prev and curr, resolve_state(None, curr_val) returns local_val=None (conflict_resolver.py:28-49) -> changed[field]=None -> an outbound update that CLEARS status/assignee/priority/title for every key present in both, EVERY pass = DATA LOSS, *IF _emit_both is authoritative*.
- Sizing agent claim: branch-3 output is DROPPED at _mutation_to_batch_dict (no changed_fields key) -> harmless empty no-op. => narrowing safe.
- Dossier A: did NOT confirm that batch-conversion trace; flags _emit_both (differ.py:556-607, fed run_differs.py:198-200) as THE fence. resolve_state returns the prev value for FIELD_CLASSES status/assignee/priority/title/type.
RESOLUTION REQUIRED (pick one, verified by a corpus mutation-diff prev-full vs prev-keyset): (i) PROVE _emit_both outbound updates are superseded/dropped by the binding-aware compute_outbound_mutations (run_differs.py:456-467) — differ.py:7-11 calls the (prev,next) form "the LEGACY snapshot-diff contract it replaces"; OR (ii) make the snapshot-differ skip the both-path for empty-value prev entries (route to create/adopt); OR (iii) stop feeding prev as local_state to compute_mutations entirely.
ALSO verify: branch-(2) neutering (outbound-CREATE for keys in prev-not-curr; branch-2 payload DOES reach Jira) — narrowing makes it emit nothing; this is very likely a CORRECTNESS IMPROVEMENT aligning with ADR 0028 (absence != deletion; the level-triggered binding-walk already covers bound-but-absent via direct GET), but confirm via the same corpus diff.
